Salmonid Mortality Analysis: TidyTuesday

Reproducible analysis using quarto

Arush Mohan

Published

23 March 2026

1 Overview

Add an overview of the project

2 How does monthly mortality differ over time?

A summary of mortality statistics

Code 
# Line plot showing mortality over time by species.
ggplot(mort_time, aes(x = date, y = median_mortality, colour = species)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Median Monthly Mortality Over Time",
    subtitle = "Aggregated across all regions",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Median mortality"
  ) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 year", date_labels = "%Y") +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

3 Which region has the lowest mortality?

Average mortality for each region - overall lowest 15

Code 
# The dataset does not contain raw individual‑fish mortality measurements, only regional monthly medians

# Average median mortality by region
#Since the dataset contains country, county, and area level data, I will filter to include only counties

mort_by_region <- monthly_mortality_data |> 
  mutate(date = as.Date(date)) |> 
  filter(geo_group == "county") |> 
  group_by(region, species) |> 
  summarise(
    avg_median_mortality = mean(median, na.rm = TRUE), #avg of medians
    p10 = quantile(median, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),
    p90 = quantile(median, .90, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_months = sum(!is.na(median)), #count of months
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Select the 15 regions with the lowest average mortality.
lowest_regions <- mort_by_region |>
  arrange(avg_median_mortality) |>
  slice_head(n = 15)

lowest_regions
# A tibble: 9 × 6
  region                       species avg_median_mortality   p10   p90 n_months
  <chr>                        <chr>                  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>    <int>
1 Nordland                     salmon                 0.427 0.311 0.54        72
2 Troms                        salmon                 0.487 0.246 0.719       72
3 Finnmark                     salmon                 0.550 0.243 0.88        72
4 Trøndelag                    salmon                 0.661 0.423 0.859       72
5 Rogaland & Vestland          rainbo…                0.678 0.491 0.927       72
6 Møre og Romsdal              salmon                 0.706 0.38  1.10        72
7 Agder & Rogaland             salmon                 0.716 0.46  1.02        72
8 Møre og Romsdal, Trøndelag,… rainbo…                0.749 0.381 1.19        72
9 Vestland                     salmon                 0.796 0.511 1.17        72

Plotting mortality by region

Code 
# Filtering for just salmon
#salmon_lowest_region <- lowest_regions |> 
#  filter(species == "salmon")

# Filtering for just rainbow trout
#trout_lowest_region <- lowest_regions |> 
#  filter(species == "rainbowtrout")

# Salmon lowest mortality graph
ggplot(lowest_regions,
       aes(x = reorder(region, avg_median_mortality),
           y = avg_median_mortality,
           fill = species)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Counties with Lowest Average Median Mortality",
    subtitle = "Filtered to geo_group = 'County'",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Average median mortality"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

4 What other types of losses are significant?

The losses dataset includes:

  • dead
  • discarded
  • escaped
  • other

We reshape the dataset to explore proportions.

Code 
#|label: loss long data
#|include: false

# Convert wide columns (dead, discarded, escaped, other)
# into a long tidy format: loss_type, count.
loss_long <- monthly_losses_data |>
  mutate(date = as.Date(date)) |>
  select(species, date, geo_group, region, losses, dead, discarded, escaped, other) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(dead, discarded, escaped, other),
    names_to = "loss_type",
    values_to = "count"
  ) |>
  mutate(share = count / losses)   # compute proportion of total loss

loss_long
# A tibble: 11,232 × 8
   species date       geo_group region losses loss_type  count    share
   <chr>   <date>     <chr>     <chr>   <dbl> <chr>      <dbl>    <dbl>
 1 salmon  2020-01-01 area      1       31425 dead       28126 0.895   
 2 salmon  2020-01-01 area      1       31425 discarded   3299 0.105   
 3 salmon  2020-01-01 area      1       31425 escaped        0 0       
 4 salmon  2020-01-01 area      1       31425 other          0 0       
 5 salmon  2020-01-01 area      2      324116 dead      277888 0.857   
 6 salmon  2020-01-01 area      2      324116 discarded  46113 0.142   
 7 salmon  2020-01-01 area      2      324116 escaped        0 0       
 8 salmon  2020-01-01 area      2      324116 other        115 0.000355
 9 salmon  2020-01-01 area      3      844829 dead      776983 0.920   
10 salmon  2020-01-01 area      3      844829 discarded  63770 0.0755  
# ℹ 11,222 more rows

Summarise total losses by type

Code 
# For each loss type, compute total and mean share.
loss_long |>
  group_by(loss_type) |>
  summarise(
    total_count = sum(count, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_share = mean(share, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  arrange(desc(total_count))
# A tibble: 4 × 3
  loss_type total_count avg_share
  <chr>           <dbl>     <dbl>
1 dead       1064081702  0.880   
2 other       121581138  0.0596  
3 discarded    60020557  0.0596  
4 escaped        434424  0.000354

Loss over time

Code 
# Summarise total losses by type per month.
loss_time <- loss_long |>
  group_by(loss_type, date) |>
  summarise(total = sum(count, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(loss_time,
       aes(x = date, y = total, colour = loss_type)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Loss Types Over Time",
    subtitle = "Aggregated across all regions and species",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Number of fish"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +  # prettier y-axis
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

Loss composition by region

Code 
# Compute regional loss composition (share of each type)
loss_by_region <- loss_long |>
  filter(geo_group == "county") |> 
  group_by(region, loss_type) |>
  summarise(total = sum(count, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  group_by(region) |>
  mutate(share = total / sum(total)) |>
  ungroup()

# Select the 5 regions with the highest total losses.
top_regions <- loss_by_region |>
  group_by(region) |>
  summarise(total_losses = sum(total), .groups = "drop") |>
  arrange(desc(total_losses)) |>
  slice_head(n = 5) |>
  pull(region)

# Plot the composition.
loss_by_region |>
  filter(region %in% top_regions) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(region, share), y = share, fill = loss_type)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Loss Composition by Region",
    subtitle = "Top 5 counties by total losses",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Share of total losses"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format()) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
